name: Longest Bitonic Subsequence
statement: Given an array arr of n integers, the task is to find the length of the longest bitonic sequence. A sequence is considered bitonic if it first increases, then decreases. The sequence does not have to be contiguous.
constraints:
• 1 <= arr.length <= 103
  • -106<= arr[i] <= 106

examples.explanation: The longest bitonic sequence is [1, 2, 3, 6, 8, 7] with length 6.
The entire array is bitonic, increasing up to 50 and then decreasing.
